features.name: Unified ML Platform
features.description: Manages the complete machine learning lifecycle, from custom model training to endpoint predictions.

features.name: Generative AI Agent Studio
features.description: Visual canvas to design, prototype, and evaluate conversational agents grounded in corporate data.

features.name: Model Garden Repository
features.description: Central repository to browse, test, and deploy Google's Gemini models and open-weight architectures.

iamRoles.description: Full access to administer all Vertex AI datasets, endpoints, models, and pipelines.
Permissions to run models, upload data, and execute training pipelines.

cliExamples.description: List all model deployments in Vertex AI
Get details of an active AI endpoint

design: AI ecosystems are selected to deploy predictive machine learning or autonomous generative agents.
Vertex AI is the core platform, providing unified registries for training, tuning, and prediction pipelines.
Model Garden hosts foundation models (like Gemini 3.5 Pro/Flash) and third-party weights (Llama, Claude).
Agent Development Kit (ADK) builds modular conversational workflows, managing agent tasks.
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Engine grounds LLMs with private databases, preventing hallucinations.
Model Armor acts as an inline security firewall, screening prompts and responses for injections.
Colab Enterprise runs managed Jupyter notebook environments with enterprise-grade compliance.
Integrate AI models with Cloud Monitoring to track query latency, prediction drift, and token rates.
security: Model Armor screens inputs/outputs for prompt injections, malware, and sensitive PII leaks.
VPC Service Controls perimeters protect custom training data and models from exfiltration.
Data-at-rest is encrypted by default; configure Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) on storage.
Manage model query access using specific IAM roles (`roles/aiplatform.user`), avoiding admin roles.
Private Google Access is required to allow compute runtimes to trigger AI endpoints privately.
Establish Safety Filters in Vertex AI to block hateful, explicit, or toxic model outputs.
Audit ML training jobs, endpoint query rates, and administrative changes in Cloud Audit Logs.
Restrict client access scopes on VM instances connecting to Vertex AI APIs.
reliability: Vertex AI endpoints scale automatically to meet query spikes, distributing requests across zones.
Foundation models are hosted on Google's globally redundant infrastructure, ensuring high SLAs.
Dataflow pipelines automate continuous data ingestion for retraining machine learning models.
Configure failover endpoints to route traffic to backup models during primary model latency spikes.
Save model training states (checkpoints) to Cloud Storage to resume training after worker failures.
Monitor model prediction drift and accuracy degradation using Vertex AI Model Monitoring.
Client libraries support automatic retries with backoff on model query rate limits (429 errors).
Maintain version controls on model registry artifacts to allow safe rollbacks.
cost: Charged based on custom training compute hours (node/hour), endpoint prediction volume, or token counts.
Gemini models charge per 1K tokens for input and output; Flash is significantly cheaper than Pro.
Model Armor charges a flat rate per million tokens processed after the monthly free tier.
Configure scale-down thresholds (minimum nodes to zero) for custom endpoints to save compute fees.
Use lightweight, cheaper models (Flash) for simple summarization tasks, reserving Pro for complex reasoning.
Clean up old custom model versions, datasets, and inactive Jupyter notebooks to reduce storage fees.
Establish alerts in Cloud Billing to monitor generative AI token consumption.
Use Google's pre-trained APIs (Vision, Speech-to-Text) when possible instead of training custom models.
